question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / EcrecoverRatifier.isRatified (ecrecover returning address(0))] Can an unprivileged attacker under the precondition that isAuthorized[offer.maker][address(0)] = true (maker accidentally authorized address(0)), craft a signature (v, r, s) such that ecrecover returns address(0), then call take() so that EcrecoverRatifier.isRatified passes the check require(_signer == offer.maker || IMidnight(MIDNIGHT).isAuthorized(offer.maker, _signer)) because isAuthorized[maker][address(0)] = true, violating the invariant that only the maker or their authorized signers can ratify offers, causing scoped impact: gate bypass where attacker fills any offer in any tree without a valid signature? Proof idea: set isAuthorized[maker][address(0)]=true, craft invalid sig producing address(0) from ecrecover, assert isRatified succeeds.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (multicall + fee-on-transfer loanToken)] Can an unprivileged attacker under the precondition that market.loanToken is fee-on-transfer call multicall([take_call_1, take_call_2]) so that both takes update claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] by their respective fee amounts, but each safeTransferFrom delivers less than recorded due to the transfer fee, violating the invariant that sum(claimableSettlementFee[token]) <= Midnight's actual token balance across all markets, causing scoped impact: insolvency amplified by multicall batching multiple fee-on-transfer takes? Proof idea: fuzz N takes in multicall, assert cumulative balance shortfall.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (callback reentrancy via sellerCallback calling repay)] Can an unprivileged maker under the precondition that offer.buy=false and offer.callback is set to a contract that calls repay() on behalf of the seller during onSell, trigger take() so that after safeTransferFrom delivers sellerAssets to receiver, ISellCallback(sellerCallback).onSell calls repay(market, units, seller, address(0), ''), reducing seller's debt and increasing withdrawable, then the health check at line 476 passes with the reduced debt, violating the invariant that the health check reflects the post-take debt before any repayment, causing scoped impact: unauthorized debt reduction where seller avoids health check by repaying during callback? Proof idea: deploy sellerCallback that calls repay(), assert seller passes health check with less collateral than required without callback.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (callback reentrancy via buyerCallback calling take again)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.buy=true and takerCallback is set to a contract that calls take() again with a different offer during onBuy, trigger take() so that the outer take's state changes (credit/debt, claimableSettlementFee) are partially applied, then the inner take() applies its own state changes, then the outer take() continues with stale position references, violating the invariant that position state is consistent across nested calls, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where credit/debt/withdrawable are double-counted? Proof idea: deploy buyerCallback that calls take() recursively, assert position invariants after nested take.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (callback reentrancy via sellerCallback calling liquidate)] Can an unprivileged liquidator under the precondition that offer.buy=false and offer.callback calls liquidate() on the seller during onSell, trigger take() so that the seller's liquidationLocked transient storage is set to true (line 444), preventing liquidation during the callback, but the callback attempts liquidate() which reverts with NotLiquidatable due to the lock, violating the invariant that the liquidation lock correctly prevents liquidation during take callbacks, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where liquidation of an unhealthy seller is blocked during take? Proof idea: deploy sellerCallback that calls liquidate(), assert liquidate() reverts with NotLiquidatable during callback.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (fee-on-transfer loanToken, buy offer, maxAssets cap)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.buy=true, offer.maxAssets > 0, and market.loanToken is fee-on-transfer, trigger take() so that consumed[maker][group] += buyerAssets tracks the nominal buyerAssets (not the fee-adjusted amount), while Midnight receives only buyerAssets*(1-fee), causing the offer to be considered consumed at the nominal maxAssets limit even though Midnight received less, violating the invariant that consumed[maker][group] accurately reflects actual assets received, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where offer is exhausted before maxAssets of real value is transferred? Proof idea: fuzz fee rate and maxAssets, assert consumed reaches maxAssets before Midnight receives maxAssets*(1-fee).

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (loanToken with transfer hook modifying Midnight's balance)] Can an unprivileged attacker under the precondition that market.loanToken has a transfer hook (e.g., ERC777 tokensReceived) that calls Midnight.withdraw() when tokens are sent to Midnight, trigger take() so that safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) fires the hook which calls withdraw() reducing withdrawable and credit, then the outer take() continues with stale _marketState.withdrawable, violating the invariant that marketState.withdrawable >= 0 and is consistent with lender credits, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where withdrawable underflows or lenders cannot withdraw? Proof idea: deploy ERC777 with tokensReceived calling withdraw(), assert withdrawable invariant after take.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (loanToken with transfer hook modifying consumed)] Can an unprivileged maker under the precondition that market.loanToken has a transfer hook that calls setConsumed(group, type(uint256).max, maker) when tokens are transferred, trigger take() so that after consumed[maker][group] is incremented (line 368-372), the transfer hook sets consumed[maker][group] = type(uint256).max, then the require(newConsumed <= offer.maxAssets) check uses the stale newConsumed value (already computed before the hook), violating the invariant that consumed[maker][group] accurately reflects the fill state, causing scoped impact: offer replay where maker can reset consumed to max and then fill again? Proof idea: deploy ERC777 with tokensReceived calling setConsumed, assert consumed state after take.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (callback revert after partial state update)] Can an unprivileged maker under the precondition that offer.buy=true and offer.callback is a contract that reverts in onBuy after checking that buyerPos.credit has been updated (by reading Midnight state), trigger take() so that the callback observes the updated credit/debt state (lines 408-418 have already executed), then reverts, causing take() to revert and roll back all state, violating the invariant that state changes are not observable by callbacks before the transaction completes, causing scoped impact: information leak where callback can observe and react to mid-transaction state? Proof idea: deploy callback that reads position state and reverts if credit increased, assert callback can observe state changes.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (fee-on-transfer loanToken, continuous fee accounting)] Can an unprivileged lender under the precondition that market.loanToken is fee-on-transfer and continuousFee > 0, trigger take() so that buyerPendingFeeIncrease = buyerCreditIncrease.mulDivDown(continuousFee * timeToMaturity, WAD) is computed on the nominal buyerCreditIncrease (not fee-adjusted), while the actual credit increase is based on nominal units (not fee-adjusted assets), violating the invariant that pendingFee <= credit for all positions, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where pendingFee exceeds actual credit due to fee-on-transfer discrepancy? [File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (loanToken returning true on failed transfer)] Can an unprivileged attacker under the precondition that market.loanToken is a malicious ERC20 that returns true on transferFrom even when the transfer fails (no actual balance movement), trigger take() so that SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom succeeds (returndata decodes to true), state changes are committed (credit/debt updated, claimableSettlementFee incremented), but Midnight's actual token balance does not increase, violating the invariant that Midnight's token balance increases by buyerAssets - sellerAssets on each take, causing scoped impact: direct loss where lenders receive credit without Midnight receiving tokens? [File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (loanToken returning true on failed transferFrom for repay)] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that market.loanToken returns true on transferFrom without moving tokens, call repay(market, units, onBehalf, address(0), '') so that position[id][onBehalf].debt -= units and marketState[id].withdrawable += units are committed, but safeTransferFrom(loanToken, msg.sender, address(this), units) returns true without transferring, violating the invariant that debt reduction is backed by actual token receipt, causing scoped impact: direct loss where borrower eliminates debt without paying? [File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (callback revert freezing seller's collateral)] Can an unprivileged maker under the precondition that offer.buy=false and offer.callback is a contract that always reverts in onSell, trigger take() so that after state changes (seller's debt increased, buyer's credit increased), safeTransferFrom transfers tokens, then ISellCallback(sellerCallback).onSell reverts, causing take() to revert and roll back all state, but the seller's collateral remains locked in Midnight (since supplyCollateral was called separately and is not rolled back), violating the invariant that a seller can always exit their position, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where seller's collateral is permanently locked if all sell offers have reverting callbacks? [File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (fee-on-transfer loanToken, withdrawable accounting)] Can an unprivileged lender under the precondition that market.loanToken is fee-on-transfer and a borrower has repaid (increasing withdrawable by full units but Midnight received units*(1-fee)), call withdraw(market, units, onBehalf, receiver) so that position[id][onBehalf].credit -= units and marketState[id].withdrawable -= units succeed, but safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, units) delivers only units*(1-fee) to receiver, violating the invariant that a lender with credit can withdraw exactly their credit amount, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where lenders receive less than their credit entitlement? [File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (callback revert in flashLoan)] Can an unprivileged attacker under the precondition that they call flashLoan([loanToken], [assets], callback, data) where callback.onFlashLoan returns a value other than CALLBACK_SUCCESS, trigger flashLoan() so that safeTransfer(loanToken, callback, assets) delivers tokens to callback, then IFlashLoanCallback(callback).onFlashLoan returns wrong bytes32, causing flashLoan() to revert with WrongFlashLoanCallbackReturnValue, but the safeTransfer has already been executed (tokens sent to callback), violating the invariant that flashLoan is atomic and Midnight's balance is unchanged after revert, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where tokens are stuck in callback if transfer is non-reversible? [File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (loanToken with decimals mismatch vs oracle price scale)] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that market.loanToken has 6 decimals (like USDC) but oracle prices are denominated in ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE=1e36 assuming 18-decimal loan token, create a market where hashCollateralParams commits to the oracle address, then call take() as seller to borrow units that are denominated in 6-decimal loan token units, causing isHealthy to compute maxDebt in 6-decimal units but compare against debt in 6-decimal units with oracle prices scaled for 18 decimals, violating the invariant that health checks correctly account for token decimals, causing scoped impact: bad debt where borrower borrows more than collateral value due to decimal mismatch? [File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (fee-on-transfer loanToken, bad debt socialization)] Can an unprivileged liquidator under the precondition that market.loanToken is fee-on-transfer and a borrower has bad debt, call liquidate() so that badDebt is realized (lossFactor updated, totalUnits decremented), then safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), repaidUnits) delivers only repaidUnits*(1-fee) to Midnight, while marketState[id].withdrawable += repaidUnits is incremented by the full amount, violating the invariant that withdrawable <= Midnight's actual loanToken balance, causing scoped impact: insolvency where lenders cannot withdraw their full credit after bad debt socialization? [File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (callback revert after lossFactor update)] Can an unprivileged liquidator under the precondition that they call liquidate() with a callback that reverts in onLiquidate after bad debt has been realized (lossFactor updated, totalUnits decremented, continuousFeeCredit updated), trigger liquidate() so that all bad debt accounting is applied, collateral is seized (safeTransfer to receiver), then callback.onLiquidate reverts, causing liquidate() to revert and roll back all state including the lossFactor update, violating the invariant that bad debt realization is atomic with the liquidation, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where lossFactor is temporarily updated and rolled back, potentially allowing double-liquidation? [File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (loanToken with approve race condition, USDT-style)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that market.loanToken is USDT-style (requires allowance to be 0 before setting non-zero), trigger take() so that payer must approve Midnight for buyerAssets, but if payer has a non-zero allowance from a previous partial fill, the approve call reverts, causing take() to revert, violating the invariant that a taker with sufficient balance can always fill a valid offer, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where take() is permanently DoSed for USDT-style tokens with non-zero residual allowance? [File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (callback revert in onBuy after safeTransferFrom)] Can an unprivileged maker under the precondition that offer.buy=true and offer.callback is a contract that calls onBuy successfully but then the subsequent safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) reverts (e.g., payer has insufficient balance), trigger take() so that onBuy has already executed (potentially with side effects like emitting events or updating external state), then the transfer reverts, causing take() to revert and roll back Midnight state but not the external side effects of onBuy, violating the invariant that onBuy side effects are atomic with Midnight state changes, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption in external systems that rely on onBuy events? [File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (fee-on-transfer loanToken, settlement fee rounding)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that market.loanToken is fee-on-transfer and settlementFee is non-zero, trigger take() so that claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] += buyerAssets - sellerAssets is incremented by the full fee amount, but Midnight receives only (buyerAssets - sellerAssets)*(1-transferFee) from the payer, causing claimableSettlementFee to exceed actual balance by (buyerAssets - sellerAssets)*transferFee per take, violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee[token] <= Midnight's token balance minus sum of all lender credits, causing scoped impact: insolvency where feeClaimer drains lender funds? [File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (callback revert in onRepay after debt reduction)] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that they call repay(market, units, onBehalf, callback, data) where callback.onRepay reverts after reading the updated debt state (debt already reduced in storage), trigger repay() so that position[id][onBehalf].debt -= units and marketState[id].withdrawable += units are applied, callback.onRepay observes the reduced debt, then reverts, causing repay() to revert and roll back state, violating the invariant that debt state is not observable by callbacks before the transaction completes, causing scoped impact: information leak where callback can observe mid-transaction debt state? [File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (loanToken with transfer that modifies allowance unexpectedly)] Can an unprivileged attacker under the precondition that market.loanToken has a non-standard transferFrom that does not decrement allowance (like USDT with max allowance), trigger take() so that SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom succeeds without consuming the payer's allowance, allowing the payer to be used as a perpetual funding source for multiple takes without re-approving, violating the invariant that each take consumes the payer's allowance proportionally, causing scoped impact: direct loss where payer's tokens are drained without their explicit per-take approval? [File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (fee-on-transfer collateral, withdrawCollateral)] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that collateral token is fee-on-transfer and borrower has supplied collateral (position.collateral[i] = assets but Midnight holds assets*(1-fee)), call withdrawCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets*(1-fee), onBehalf, receiver) so that position.collateral[i] -= assets*(1-fee) passes the health check, but safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, assets*(1-fee)) delivers only assets*(1-fee)^2 to receiver, violating the invariant that withdrawCollateral delivers exactly the requested assets to receiver, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where borrower cannot recover full collateral value? [File: 'src/ratifiers/libraries/HashLib.sol -> Scope: High Non-standard ERC20 behavior, fee-on-transfer behavior, callback revert behavior, or false-return handling that breaks accounting or freezes funds'] [Function: hashOffer / take (callback revert in onBuy causing lossFactor desync)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the buyer has existing credit and the market has a non-zero lossFactor, trigger take() so that _updatePosition is called for buyer (line 379), updating buyer's credit based on current lossFactor, then buyerCallback.onBuy reverts, causing take() to revert and roll back the position update, but if lossFactor changes between the revert and the next take() call (due to another liquidation in the same block), the buyer's lastLossFactor is stale, violating the invariant that lastLossFactor is always updated before credit is used, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where buyer's credit is incorrectly slashed?
